subject: Databases
title: Google Spanner
description: A short lesson to help you apply Google Spanner.

hook.text: A globally distributed SQL database with strong consistency? Google built Spanner to do exactly that.

buildup.text: Spanner uses TrueTime: GPS and atomic clocks in every data center give a globally synchronized time source.

discovery.text: With synchronized time, Spanner orders transactions globally without expensive cross-region coordination for every read.

twist.text: Spanner is the only database that offers external consistency (stronger than linearizability) at global scale.

climax.text: Cloud Spanner is the managed version. It scales horizontally while supporting SQL, schemas, and ACID transactions.

punchline.text: Spanner: global SQL, strong consistency, powered by atomic clocks.

quiz.question: What technology does Google Spanner use to achieve global strong consistency?
options: TrueTime — GPS and atomic clocks that provide synchronized time across all data centers
A single master node that coordinates all transactions
Eventually consistent replication with client-side conflict resolution
